persona: W1.1-R2 Mrs Chen (rerun on F6-fixed backend)
verdict_rationale: Session terminated with explicit termination_reason='user_input_unparseable_no_spec' (F6 fix #3 working). No HTTP 5xx, no silent zero-responses, turn_index correctly preserved on triage-reject (F6 fix #2 working). However, no spec_capsule_cid produced and only 'job' slot was ever covered (and even regressed to [] after turn 2). Session ended after 3 user answers without honoring the playbook in depth — the LLM kept re-emitting the same Voss-mirror question across turns 1 and 2 without advancing, then terminated on turn 3.

f6_fix_verifications.no_silent_zero_responses: PASS — all 4 HTTP responses were 200 with non-empty fields except final terminate (question_text='' is expected on terminate)
f6_fix_verifications.turn_index_preserved_on_triage_reject: PASS — T2 response kept turn_index=2 (did not advance from prior turn_index=2) after triage-reject of 'rerun' answer; T3 also stayed at turn_index=2 before terminate
f6_fix_verifications.termination_reason_field_present: PASS — final response includes termination_reason='user_input_unparseable_no_spec' (not null) and is not the F6-regression case of both spec_capsule_cid=null AND termination_reason=null

observations.d8_shellout_flakiness: Not observed — zero HTTP 5xx in this session.
observations.d9_voss_mirror_loop_or_slot_stall: OBSERVED — same Voss-mirror question ('听起来你是说需要一个像俄罗斯方块那样简单、不需要稳定WiFi也能玩的游戏，对吗？...') emitted on both T1 and T2 responses. covered_slots stuck at ['job'] for T0-T1 then regressed to [] for T2-T3. Per spec, this is an LLM-side prompt issue F7 addresses, NOT a F6 regression.
observations.turn_latency_anomaly: T2 response returned in 1s vs T1's 15s — consistent with triage-reject short-circuit (no LLM call), supporting F6 fix #2 behavior.

narrative: The F6-fixed backend correctly surfaced an explicit termination_reason ('user_input_unparseable_no_spec') instead of the silent terminate-with-nulls regression that F6 was meant to prevent — this is the headline fix-validation result. turn_index was also correctly preserved across the triage-reject of turn 2 (1s response with same question, no advance), matching F6 fix #2. No HTTP 5xx and no zero-question silent responses occurred. However, the session itself made no real progress: the LLM emitted the same Voss-mirror double-question on T1 and T2 (so the playbook's 'anchor' answer on T2 never landed against a fresh prompt), covered_slots peaked at ['job'] (1/7) and even regressed to [] before terminate, and the session ended at T3 after the 'scope' answer was deemed unparseable. This is the D9 slot-stall / Voss-mirror-loop pattern flagged for F7, not a F6 regression. Verdict: PARTIAL — F6 fixes verified working, but spec not captured.
